title: dalbergia matthewii
intro: Dalbergia matthewii, also known as the Indian rosewood, is a deciduous tree native to the Western Ghats of India. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dalbergia matthewii include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and may be used to treat various ailments, including fever, joint pain, and skin irritations.
